back_translation: When Sanballat, Tobiah, Geshem the Arab, and the rest of our enemies heard that I had finished building the wall, with no gap remaining — though at that time I had not yet installed the doors at the gates —
Sanballat and Geshem sent a message to me saying, "Come, let us meet together in a village on the plain of Ono." But they had a plan to harm me.
So I sent messengers to them, saying, "I am doing a great work and cannot come down. Why should the work stop when I leave it to go down to you?"
They sent this message to me four times, and I replied the same way each time.
The fifth time, Sanballat sent the same message to me by his young servant, who had in his hand an unsealed letter,

in which was written, "It is reported among the nations — and Geshem agrees — that you and the Jews are planning to revolt, and therefore you are building the wall. And according to these reports, you are going to set yourself up as their king,
you have also appointed prophets in Jerusalem to proclaim concerning you, saying, 'There is a king in Judah.' Soon these rumors will reach the ear of the king. Come, let us confer together."
So I sent a reply to him saying, "What you say has never happened; you are inventing it from your own heart."
For they were all trying to frighten us, saying, "Their hands will grow weak from the work, and it will not succeed." But now, O my God, please strengthen my hands.

Afterward I went to the house of Shemaiah son of Delaiah son of Mehetabel, who was confined at home. He said, "Let us meet at the house of God inside the temple, and let us shut the doors of the temple, because they are coming to kill you — this very night they are coming to kill you."
But I answered, "Should a man like me run away? Who like me would enter the temple to save his life? I will not enter."
Then I realized that God had not sent him, but he had spoken this prophecy against me because Tobiah and Sanballat had hired him.
He had been hired so that I would be afraid and act accordingly, and would sin — so that they could use it to give me a bad name in order to discredit me.
O my God, remember Tobiah and Sanballat according to these deeds of theirs, and also Noadiah the prophetess and the rest of the prophets who tried to make me afraid.
So the wall was finished and completed in fifty-two days, on the twenty-fifth of the month of Elul.
When all our enemies heard, all the nations around us became afraid and discouraged, because they knew that this work had been accomplished by our God.
Also in those days, the nobles of Judah sent many letters to Tobiah, and Tobiah's letters kept coming back to them.
For many in Judah were bound to him by oath, because he was the son-in-law of Shecaniah son of Arah, and Jehohanan his son had married the daughter of Meshullam son of Berechiah.
Moreover, these nobles kept telling me of Tobiah's good deeds, and they reported my words back to him. And Tobiah sent letters to make me afraid.
